chunk_summary: A minimum feasible WASH prescription for stunting clinics in high-burden settings focuses on safer drinking water, hand hygiene, and child feces/play-space control to reduce enteric exposure, diarrhea, and inflammation-driven growth suppression.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of prioritized WASH behaviors in reducing stunting and chronic undernutrition.
Learn the three key components of a minimum feasible WASH prescription for stunting clinics.
Recognize the importance of linking diarrhea prevention to illness management with ORS and zinc supplementation.
key_facts: Large trials like WASH Benefits and SHINE showed limited direct effects on linear growth but highlighted the clinical value of reducing enteric exposure.

Three priorities: safer drinking water (protected source, safe storage, boiling/chlorination), hand hygiene (soap use after defecation and before food handling), and child feces/play-space control (toilet disposal, clean play mat).
Every diarrhea episode should trigger early ORS plus zinc supplementation and review of WASH practices.
Audit achievable WASH changes at follow-up, e.g., 'covered water container started' or 'soap station placed near toilet'.
Frequent diarrhea may indicate issues like open drains, unsafe water storage, or contaminated play areas.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Large trials like WASH Benefits and SHINE showed limited direct effects on linear growth but emphasized the importance of reducing enteric exposure to lower diarrhea and infection burden.
document_key_topics: Minimum feasible WASH prescription for stunting clinics
Impact of WASH interventions on stunting and chronic undernutrition
Diarrhea prevention and management in high-burden settings
